title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dry eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extract from the plant's flowers, known as Echinacea pallida, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, including in the eyes. This anti-inflammatory effect can help alleviate dry eye symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and burning sensations. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower tinctures can help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing discomfort and promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Kansas Coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms such as redness and irritation.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in Kansas Coneflower tincture help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye disease.

Immunomodulatory: Kansas Coneflower's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune response in the eyes, reducing inflammation and preventing excessive mucus production that can further exacerbate dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and irritation in dry eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the Kansas coneflower plant have been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them an effective part for making medicinal tinctures for dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Clean and chop the botanicals into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one part chopped echinacea angustifolia with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all plant material is covered.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily, allowing the herbal extract to mature and develop its properties.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids and preserving the liquid extract for use as eyedrops.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with dry eyes because its extracts contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tannins and flavonoids in maidenhair tree work to reduce redness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with dry eye syndrome. Additionally, the tincture's ability to improve ocular blood flow helps to nourish and hydrate the eyes, reducing discomfort and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The tincture may help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Maidenhair tree tincture may reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is a common underlying cause of dry eye disease.
Tissue-protective properties: Some studies suggest that Adiantum extracts may have protective effects on corneal and conjunctival tissues, potentially helping to repair and maintain ocular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help soothe and protect the eyes from dryness and irritation.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in reducing eye inflammation and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are at their peak potency and freshness.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with clean water and gently pat them dry with a paper towel.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and combine them with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, using a ratio of one part herb to three parts solvent.
Allow the mixture to steep for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean container.
Store the finished tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid and use it within one year of preparation, taking 20-30 drops three times a day as needed to relieve dry eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms irritated eye tissues. The tincture's high concentration of allantoin reduces redness and swelling, while its antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress and damage caused by prolonged screen use or environmental factors. As a result, comfrey tinctures provide fast and effective relief from dry, itchy, and tired eyes, promoting healthy vision and overall ocular well-being.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: This compound helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the eyes, which can alleviate dryness and discomfort caused by conditions like blepharitis or meibomian gland dysfunction.

Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that help to reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, which can provide relief from redness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with dry eyes.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids are antioxidants that help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms such as blurred vision and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots are used due to their high concentration of allantoin, a compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of comfrey plants, contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest symphytum officinale leaves and flowers, ensure freshness by using immediately or drying within hours.
Chop 20 parts of dried comfrey leaf and flower into small pieces to release essential oils.
Combine chopped comfrey with 80% vodka or high-proof grain spirit in a clean glass jar, fill to the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily to macerate the mixture gently.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discard solids and label dated tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The natural gel of aloe vera has been used for centuries to treat various eye issues, including dryness and itchiness. When applied as a tincture, the aloe's moisturizing and regenerative effects penetrate deep into the eye, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and dryness. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial properties help prevent infections that can exacerbate dry eyes, promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate dry eye symptoms like redness and swelling.
Moisturizing: The gel-like substance present in herbal aloe tinctures helps to moisturize and lubricate the eyes, providing instant relief from dryness and discomfort.
Antioxidant: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic dry eye conditions and promote overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain aloin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them ideal for treating dry eyes.
Barks: The barks of the aloe plant contain saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from mature plants, ensuring they are free of any signs of mold or decay.
Remove the outer rind and pulp from the leaves, retaining only the clear gel-like substance within.
Weigh 100 grams of the aloe vera gel and combine it with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and preserving the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains berberine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When used topically, goldenseal tincture reduces inflammation and irritation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of dry eye syndrome such as redness, itchiness, and burning sensations. Its antimicrobial properties also help to combat bacterial overgrowth that can contribute to dry eye conditions. By soothing and healing the eyes, goldenseal tinctures provide relief from discomfort and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key constituent of goldenseal, has antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and infection in the eyes, which may contribute to dry eye symptoms.

Sennosides: The sennoside content in goldenseal tincture acts as a natural demulcent, coating the eyes with a protective film that soothes and moisturizes the surface, providing relief from dryness and irritation.
Berberin's anti-inflammatory effects: Berberine has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the eyes associated with dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to soothe and protect dry, irritated eyes.
Barks: Contain berberine and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healthy eye function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh goldenseal roots hydrastis canadensis of sufficient quantity for the desired yield.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine chopped roots with high-proof vodka at a 1:4 ratio by weight and steep in glass jars for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding solids after 2 hours.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep refrigerated for up to 2 years taking 10-20 drops orally twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbal extract contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that work to reduce redness and puffiness, providing instant relief from dry, itchy eyes. Additionally, eyebright's natural astringent properties help to reduce excess tear production, allowing the eyes to function properly and maintaining healthy tear flow. This results in a reduction of symptoms associated with dry eye syndrome, such as blurred vision and burning sensations.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry eyes, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a healthy ocular environment.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants in eyebright tincture neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and contribute to dry eye symptoms, protecting the cornea and conjunctiva from oxidative stress.

Mucilaginous: Eyebright's mucilaginous properties help soothe and lubricate the eyes by coating the surface of the eyeball with a protective film, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and glycosides, the roots of Eyebright have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe dry, irritated eyes.
Leaves: The leaves contain anthocyanins and quercetin, which provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits to alleviate dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and stems or dried euphrasia officinalis herb in the morning for maximum potency.
Chop 20 grams of euphrasia officinalis herb into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped euphrasia officinalis with 100 milliliters of 95% ethanol vodka and let it steep at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a glass bottle, discarding solids after 24 hours and store in a cool dark place.
After 6 months allow tincture to mature before filtering again and transferring to small dropper bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with dry eyes because it contains flavonoids and carotenoids that provide antioxidant properties, soothing and nourishing the ocular surface. The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold tincture also reduce redness and discomfort associated with dry eyes. Additionally, its emollient properties help to moisturize and protect the eyes, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy tear film. As a result, using pot marigold tinctures can provide fast and effective relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to dryness and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms.
Emollient and moisturizing properties: Pot marigold's emollients and mucilages, such as gallic acid and saponins, help soothe and lubricate the eyes, providing relief from dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of pot marigold are used to create a soothing and calming tincture that can help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with dry eyes.
Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in carotenoids, which can be extracted into a tincture that helps to nourish and protect the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in late morning or early afternoon.
Dry the flowers using a food dehydrator at 105°f for 6 hours or air drying.
Combine one part dried flowers with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar and let it sit.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 4 weeks shaking daily allowing the herbal goodness to infuse.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissue. The bitter compounds in dandelion extract also help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyelids and conjunctiva, allowing for improved tear drainage and reduced stinging sensation. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy vision and overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tincture's diuretic effects can help reduce inflammation and fluid retention in the body, which can alleviate dry eye symptoms by improving overall hydration levels.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant are used to make tinctures for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eye syndrome.

Seeds: The seeds of the dandelion plant contain compounds that have been shown to possess anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, making them useful in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves, collecting approximately one pound of plant material.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for at least eight hours.
Chop dried dandelion flowers and leaves into small pieces to facilitate extraction, then combine with 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the plant material to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid tincture in an amber glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbal extract reduces redness and itchiness associated with dry eye syndrome, while also promoting healthy tear production. Additionally, St John's Wort's natural antibacterial and antiviral compounds help to combat underlying infections that can exacerbate the condition. By using a high-quality tincture, individuals can experience relief from dry, itchy, and gritty eyes, leading to improved vision and overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort helps the body adapt to stress and promotes overall well-being, which can reduce eye strain and dryness caused by prolonged screen time or other stressful activities.
Antioxidant properties: The herb's antioxidants can help protect the eyes from oxidative damage and inflammation, reducing the risk of dry eye syndrome and promoting healthy tear production.
Anti-inflammatory properties: St. John's Wort's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and irritation associated with dry eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of St John's Wort contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve ocular health, making them beneficial for dry eye relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves in the summer morning, selecting only those with bright yellow petals.
Wash the harvested plant material gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris thoroughly.
Combine 100g of the cleaned plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all plant parts are completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking it daily for the first week and weekly thereafter to facilitate extraction effectively.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the tincture with an alcohol concentration of at least 25% by volume.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture's flavonoids and terpenes work to soothe and calm the eyelids, reducing redness and irritation caused by dry eye syndrome. Additionally, chamomile's natural anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling in the eyes, providing fast and effective relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eye syndrome, promoting comfort and soothing the affected area.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage tear ducts and contribute to dry eye symptoms, protecting the delicate tissues of the eyes and promoting healthy tear production.

Soothing and calming property: The apigenin in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, inducing a sense of calm and relaxation, which can help alleviate stress-induced dry eye symptoms and promote a more comfortable visual experience.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are a popular choice for making chamomile tinctures, as they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and soothe dry, irritated eyes.
Roots: Chamomile roots are used to create a more sedative and calming tincture, which can help to reduce eye fatigue and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and clean them thoroughly with filtered water.
Combine one part dried or four parts fresh flowers with 20% ethanol-based vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for six hours in a cool dark place to allow for gentle extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids immediately.
Store the herbal tincture in an amber glass bottle with tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dry eyes are concentrated liquid extracts made from various plant-based ingredients, specifically designed to soothe and nourish the eyes. These tinctures help alleviate symptoms of dry eyes by providing essential oils, antioxidants, and anti-inflammatory compounds that moisturize and protect the ocular surface. Examples include Eyebright, which reduces redness and inflammation, and Marshmallow Root, which lubricates and comforts the eyes. By using herbal tinctures, individuals with dry eyes can enjoy improved vision, reduced discomfort, and enhanced daily living activities such as reading, computer use, and even driving at night.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry eyes with herbal tinctures is to combine a few potent ingredients. Try mixing equal parts of calendula, chamomile, and eyebright tinctures in a 20-30 drop dose, taken three times daily. Calendula soothes irritation, chamomile reduces inflammation, and eyebright increases tear production. For optimal results, use high-quality tinctures and consult with an herbalist or healthcare professional for personalized guidance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures are various forms of conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and meibomian gland dysfunction. Herbs like golden seal, calendula, and chamomile can help soothe irritated eyes, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. Other herbs like peppermint, tea tree, and lavender can also provide relief from dryness, itching, and redness associated with these conditions. Tinctures can be applied topically or taken orally to provide effective natural remedies for eye health issues.
